purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This file defines the data model and business metrics for order items in a food service business, including revenue calculations, cost tracking, and profitability analysis. It establishes the semantic meaning of order item data and defines key performance indicators for business reporting.
purpose_statements.evidence: Models section defines order_items table structure with relationships; semantic_models section establishes business meaning of order items with measures for revenue calculations; metrics section contains 10+ business metrics including simple (revenue, food_revenue), ratio (food_revenue_pct), derived (revenue_growth_mom), and cumulative metrics; unit_tests validate supply cost calculations

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This mart model aggregates order data with item-level details to create a comprehensive view of each order including cost summaries, food/drink item counts, and customer ordering sequence. It serves as a centralized dataset for downstream reporting and analysis of order behavior and customer purchasing patterns.
purpose_statements.evidence: CTE structure with order_items_summary performing supply cost and product price aggregations, boolean computations for food/drink order classification, and customer_order_count using window functions for customer ordering sequence

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This file defines a data mart for order analytics, containing order-level metrics and dimensions used for business reporting. It includes logic for categorizing orders as food or drink orders, calculating totals, and defining semantic models for downstream metrics.
purpose_statements.evidence: models.orders.columns.is_food_order, models.orders.columns.is_drink_order, unit_tests.test_order_items_compute_to_bools_correctly, semantic_models.orders.measures.order_total, metrics.food_orders, metrics.drink_orders

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This file defines a customer data mart that aggregates lifetime order and spending metrics per customer, supporting customer segmentation and lifetime value analysis.
purpose_statements.evidence: models.customers.columns.lifetime_spend_pretax, models.customers.columns.customer_type, semantic_models.customers.measures.lifetime_spend, metrics.lifetime_spend_pretax, metrics.average_order_value

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This model creates a customer-level summary table that enriches customer data with order history metrics including lifetime spend, order frequency, and customer type classification. It serves as a foundational dataset for customer analytics and segmentation.
purpose_statements.evidence: CTE customer_orders_summary calculates lifetime metrics (count_lifetime_orders, lifetime_spend); case statement classifies customers as 'returning' or 'new'; joins stg_customers with aggregated order data

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This mart model aggregates order item data with related order, product, and supply cost information to provide a comprehensive view of ordered items including their financial and descriptive attributes.
purpose_statements.evidence: CTEs for order_items, orders, products, supplies; join logic combining these entities; calculation of supply_cost in order_supplies_summary; selection of business-relevant fields like product_name, product_price, is_food_item, is_drink_item

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order data by renaming fields, converting currency amounts from cents to dollars, and truncating timestamps to daily granularity. It serves as an intermediate layer between raw order data and downstream analytics models.
purpose_statements.evidence: Field renaming (id to order_id, store_id to location_id), currency conversion macros (cents_to_dollars), date truncation (dbt.date_trunc), and selection of specific columns for transformation

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This semantic model defines the product dimension for analytics, providing a structured representation of product data with associated metadata and classification attributes. It serves as a foundational data model for product-related metrics and dimensional analysis.
purpose_statements.evidence: semantic_models.name: products, model: ref('products'), entities.product.type: primary, dimensions including product_name, product_type, is_food_item, is_drink_item

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This semantic model defines the supplies dimension table structure for analytics, establishing the foundational data relationships and attributes for supply chain analysis. It provides the dimensional context needed to analyze supply-related metrics by mapping logical business concepts to physical database columns.
purpose_statements.evidence: semantic_models key with supplies model definition, entities section defining supply_uuid as primary entity, dimensions section listing supply_id, product_id, supply_name, supply_cost, and is_perishable_supply as categorical dimensions

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This SQL macro provides a standardized way to convert cent values to dollar amounts across different database platforms, ensuring consistent decimal precision and rounding behavior. It abstracts away database-specific syntax differences for monetary conversions.
purpose_statements.evidence: Macro name 'cents_to_dollars' with adapter dispatch pattern, database-specific implementations for postgres, bigquery, and fabric, numeric casting with 16,2 precision, division by 100 conversion factor

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This macro generates schema names for database objects based on resource type, environment, and custom naming rules. It handles different cases for seeds, default schemas, production vs non-production environments with custom schema names.
purpose_statements.evidence: generate_schema_name macro with conditional logic using node.resource_type, custom_schema_name, and target.name to determine schema naming patterns including seed handling, default schemas, and production environment prefixing

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This staging model transforms raw supply data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format for downstream analytics. It cleans and renames fields while adding calculated columns like supply UUID and cost conversions.
purpose_statements.evidence: source CTE references {{ source('ecom', 'raw_supplies') }}, surrogate key generation with dbt_utils.generate_surrogate_key(['id', 'sku']), cost conversion with {{ cents_to_dollars('cost') }}, field renaming (id→supply_id, sku→product_id, name→supply_name), boolean transformation (perishable→is_perishable_supply)

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This staging model transforms raw product data from the e-commerce system into a standardized format for downstream analytics use. It cleans and renames fields, converts price units from cents to dollars, and adds boolean flags to categorize products by type.
purpose_statements.evidence: sku as product_id, {{ cents_to_dollars('price') }} as product_price, coalesce(type = 'jaffle', false) as is_food_item, coalesce(type = 'beverage', false) as is_drink_item

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This staging model transforms raw store data into a standardized format for downstream analytics use. It cleans and renames fields from the raw stores table while preserving all records for further processing.
purpose_statements.evidence: CTE 'source' references {{ source('ecom', 'raw_stores') }}, CTE 'renamed' performs field mappings (id→location_id, name→location_name), uses dbt.date_trunc('day', 'opened_at') for date processing

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This staging model transforms raw customer data from the e-commerce source by renaming columns to establish consistent naming conventions for downstream consumption. It extracts customer identifiers and names from the raw customers table and presents them in a standardized format.
purpose_statements.evidence: The SQL code shows a clear pattern of selecting from source('ecom', 'raw_customers') and renaming columns (id to customer_id, name to customer_name) which represents basic data transformation logic. The CTE structure with 'source' and 'renamed' demonstrates the staging pattern of isolating source extraction from transformation logic.

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This staging model transforms raw e-commerce order item data by renaming and selecting key identifier fields to create a standardized view of order items. It serves as a foundational layer that maps raw system identifiers to business-friendly column names for downstream consumption.
purpose_statements.evidence: CTE named 'renamed' performs field mapping (id as order_item_id, sku as product_id), SELECT * from source pattern, source() function referencing ecom.raw_items

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This is the main configuration file for a dbt project named 'jaffle_shop' that defines project metadata, file paths, and model configurations. It specifies how data models should be materialized and sets up seed data loading with schema definitions.
purpose_statements.evidence: config-version, name, model-paths, seeds configuration with schema and enabled conditions, models section with staging/view and marts/table materialization patterns

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This staging model defines the structure and validation for cleaned location data, ensuring each location has a unique ID and non-null values, while also including unit tests to verify proper date truncation of opened timestamps.
purpose_statements.evidence: models.stg_locations.columns.data_tests (not_null, unique), unit_tests.test_does_location_opened_at_trunc_to_date

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: Defines a semantic model for location data that serves as a dimension table containing location information with associated tax rates. Provides analytical context for location-based metrics and time-based aggregations.
purpose_statements.evidence: semantic_models array with location entity, dimensions including location_name and opened_date, measures for average_tax_rate with aggregation

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This staging model defines the structure and validation rules for order item data, representing individual food and drink items within customer orders. It establishes the relationship between order items and their parent orders while ensuring data quality through validation constraints.
purpose_statements.evidence: YAML keys: models, name: stg_order_items, description: Individual food and drink items that make up our orders, data_tests: not_null, unique, relationships, ref('stg_orders')

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This staging model defines the structure and validation rules for cleaned order data, ensuring data quality through tests on order totals and unique identifiers.
purpose_statements.evidence: models.stg_orders.description, data_tests.expression_is_true, columns.order_id.data_tests.not_null, columns.order_id.data_tests.unique

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This model generates a time spine table containing a continuous sequence of dates for use in metric calculations and time-based analytics. It creates a standardized date dimension spanning approximately 10 years (3650 days) starting from a base date.
purpose_statements.evidence: dbt_date.get_base_dates function call with n_dateparts=365*10 parameter, cast_to_date transformation, days CTE

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This staging model defines the structure and validation rules for supply expense data, ensuring data quality through uniqueness and not-null constraints on the supply UUID. It represents cleaned supply cost records where each row corresponds to a specific cost instance rather than a supply item.
purpose_statements.evidence: YAML keys: models, name: stg_supplies, description field explaining one row per supply cost, supply_uuid column with not_null and unique data_tests

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This YAML configuration file defines the structure and validation rules for a staging table containing cleaned customer data. It establishes the schema definition for customer records with uniqueness and non-null constraints on the primary identifier.
purpose_statements.evidence: YAML keys: models, name: stg_customers, description field defining 'Customer data with basic cleaning', columns with customer_id as unique key, data_tests including not_null and unique constraints

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This YAML configuration file defines the structure and validation rules for a staging layer product data model. It establishes the schema for product information including unique identifiers and applies data quality tests to ensure integrity.
purpose_statements.evidence: YAML keys: models, name: stg_products, description field defining product data scope, columns definition with product_id, data_tests array with not_null and unique constraints

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This YAML configuration file defines the source data structure for an e-commerce system, mapping raw database tables to business entities like customers, orders, items, stores, products, and supplies. It serves as a data source declaration that connects to the raw schema containing Jaffle Shop's e-commerce transaction data.
purpose_statements.evidence: YAML keys: version, sources; source name: ecom; schema: raw; table names: raw_customers, raw_orders, raw_items, raw_stores, raw_products, raw_supplies; loaded_at_field properties for orders and stores tables

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This configuration file defines pre-commit hooks that automatically validate and format code before commits are made. It ensures code quality standards by running checks for YAML syntax, file formatting, and Python code style.
purpose_statements.evidence: YAML keys: repos, repo URLs pointing to pre-commit-hooks and ruff-pre-commit, hook IDs including check-yaml, end-of-file-fixer, trailing-whitespace, requirements-txt-fixer, ruff-check, and ruff-format

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This model serves as a location mart that provides a clean, unified view of location data for downstream consumption. It acts as an intermediate layer between raw staging data and final reporting tables.
purpose_statements.evidence: CTE named 'locations' selects all columns from stg_locations reference, final select returns all location records

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This model serves as a product mart that provides a clean, unified view of product data for reporting and analysis. It acts as a semantic layer that exposes standardized product information to downstream consumers.
purpose_statements.evidence: CTE named 'products' selects from stg_products reference, final select * from products pattern indicates minimal transformation

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This model creates a final presentation layer table for supplies data by selecting all records from the staging supplies table. It serves as a clean, denormalized view of supply information for reporting and analysis purposes.
purpose_statements.evidence: CTE named 'supplies' selects from ref('stg_supplies'), final select statement returns all columns from supplies CTE

purpose_statements.purpose_statement: This Taskfile.yml defines automation tasks for setting up a data environment, generating sample data, loading it into a database, and cleaning up resources. It orchestrates the workflow for data generation and seeding using dbt and Python virtual environments.
purpose_statements.evidence: YAML keys: version, silent, vars, YEARS, DB, tasks, venv, install; task definitions for venv, install, gen, seed, clean, load; commands for python venv activation, pip install, jafgen data generation, dbt seed execution

domains.description: Core business intelligence for order processing, revenue analysis, and profitability tracking across food and drink orders.
domains.reasoning: These files share key concepts around order aggregation, revenue calculation, food/drink classification, and profitability analysis, forming a cohesive order analytics domain.

domains.description: Customer lifecycle management including segmentation, lifetime value analysis, and purchasing behavior tracking.
domains.reasoning: Both files focus on customer-level metrics, lifetime value calculation, segmentation, and order history aggregation for customer analytics.

domains.description: Product catalog management and supply chain analytics including cost tracking and dimensional modeling.
domains.reasoning: These files handle product dimensions, supply chain analytics, cost tracking, and entity relationships for product and supply management.

domains.description: Raw data transformation, cleaning, and standardization from source systems to analytics-ready formats.
domains.reasoning: All staging models perform data transformation, cleaning, field renaming, and standardization from raw e-commerce source data.

domains.description: Project configuration, automation, and reusable utilities for the data platform.
domains.reasoning: These files handle project configuration, reusable macros, automation tasks, and code quality controls rather than business logic.

domains.description: Data quality assurance through schema definitions, validation rules, and testing frameworks.
domains.reasoning: These YAML files define data validation rules, schema definitions, and quality tests for staging models and source data.

domains.description: Store location data management including tax rate analytics and location-based metrics.
domains.reasoning: Both files handle location dimension modeling, tax rate analytics, and location-based aggregations.

domains.description: Time dimension management for metric calculations and date-based analytics.
domains.reasoning: This standalone model generates time spine tables specifically for time-based metric calculations and analytics.
